question_groups.prompt_html:
Questions 1–10

Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.

How to become a teataster

What the training involves
• A trainee tea taster mainly works in the company's 1 …………..

• He/she learns how to:
- taste the tea using a 2 ……………..
- give a 3 …………… to samples of tea according to their quality
- select the right size of leaves for teabags
- remember the 4 …………….. of different kinds of tea.

• He/she only qualifies after 5 …………….. of training.

What the tea company is looking for in new staff
• a degree ( 6 …………… is an advantage)

• able to do 7 ………….. quickly

• ability to distinguish different kinds of 8 ……………… accurately

• effective 9 ………….. skills in different situations

• being prepared to 10 ……………….

question_groups.prompt_html:
Questions 11–14

Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

Pioneer Historical Village

11 When did Pioneer Historical Village open to the public?

A 1962

B 1970

C 1980

12 How much will each adult in the group pay for a ticket?

A $11.00

B $12.50

C $16.00

13 What sort of demonstration will the visitors be able to watch?

A an unusual approach to baking bread

B a quick way of making candles

C a traditional method of making sweets

14 What kind of experience with animals can visitors have at Pioneer Village?

A gathering eggs

B milking cows

C feeding goats

items.prompt_html: 11 When did Pioneer Historical Village open to the public?
12 How much will each adult in the group pay for a ticket?
13 What sort of demonstration will the visitors be able to watch?

options.text: an unusual approach to baking bread

options.label: B
options.text: a quick way of making candles

options.label: C
options.text: a traditional method of making sweets

question_groups.prompt_html:

21-22 Which TWO things do Annie and Ryan agree are surprising about people's behaviour and attitudes to waste disposal?

A People like to be reminded about what to recycle.

B People often forget what is dangerous.

C People are prepared to pay more for safer products.

D People recycle cheap items less often than expensive ones.

E People think waste disposal is boring.

question_groups.instruction:
What advice do Ryan and Annie agree should be given about each of the following types of waste?
Choose the correct answer from the box.
question_groups.prompt_html:
Questions 25–30

Advice
A should be disposed of more regularly
B should have better labelling
C should normally be used up completely
D should be saved for later disposal
E should be made available for others' use
F should be repaired rather than thrown away
G should be further modified to be made less harmful
H should be retained and used for different purposes

27 some medicines
28 pesticides
29 tyres
30 fridges

Whale Shark Conservation Field Trip

Background
• Whale sharks are the world's biggest fish.

• Comparison with whales:
- Similarities: They're large and they feed on plankton and krill.
- Differences: They stay near the 31 ………… and they lack any actual 32 …………. .

• A large number head to the waters off Ningaloo, Western Australia, each year.

• The coral reef, where the whale sharks feed, is unusually close to the 33 …………. .

Research centre activities
• Existing methods of monitoring whale sharks are imperfect:
- tracking devices attached to the whale shark's 34 ………… (these are expensive and unreliable)
- from a 35 ………… (this is imprecise)

• New research project on whale shark feeding habits:
- Analysis of plankton shows different amounts of 36 ……… varieties.
- Researchers are comparing plankton data using special maps.

• Study on the impact of humans on whale shark behaviour: noise caused whale sharks to 37 ………….

• Community outreach programme - The whale shark race:
- A mature whale is tagged and assigned to a 38 ……….. to follow.
- Results are displayed on the research centre website.

Conservation and management
• Record any 39 ……. to whale sharks in photo-identification libraries.

• Ban scuba activities as whale sharks are affected by the 40 …………… the activities produce.
